title: Decimal place value with regrouping
description: Practice your decimal place value understanding by using place value to describe decimal numbers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write a decimal number that is equivalent to $37$ tenths.**

$37\text{ tenths} =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Let's use a picture to help us.

Each square below is split into $10$ equal pieces, or *tenths*. 
There are $\greenD{37 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded.

![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All ten rectangles are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54dc0a6d55eb50c837a2be9bf6dbee9874e7f705) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All ten rectangles are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54dc0a6d55eb50c837a2be9bf6dbee9874e7f705) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All ten rectangles are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54dc0a6d55eb50c837a2be9bf6dbee9874e7f705) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Seven rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 3 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fc92a49b03de5a209e2a5e49e7cc306a416749e2)

We can express $\greenD{37\text { tenths}}$ as $\blueD{3 \text{ ones}}$ and $\greenD{7 \text{ tenths}}$.

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Seven rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 3 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fc92a49b03de5a209e2a5e49e7cc306a416749e2)

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{3 \text{ ones}}$ and $\greenD{7 \text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD{3}$|$.$  | $\greenD7$ |
$37\text{ tenths}  =\blueD{3}.\greenD{7}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write a decimal number that is equivalent to $24$ tenths.**

$24\text{ tenths}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Let's use a picture to help us.

Each square below is split into $10$ equal pieces, or *tenths*. There are $\greenD{24 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded.

![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All 10 rectangles are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54dc0a6d55eb50c837a2be9bf6dbee9874e7f705) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All 10 rectangles are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54dc0a6d55eb50c837a2be9bf6dbee9874e7f705) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Four rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 6 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b11f8d5bd09e5dbc2f86e7437ada297a92e0f4b)

We can also express $\greenD{24 \text{ tenths}}$ as $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$ and $\greenD{4 \text{ tenths}}$.

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Four rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 6 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b11f8d5bd09e5dbc2f86e7437ada297a92e0f4b)

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$ and $\greenD{4 \text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD{2}$|$.$  | $\greenD4$ |
$24\text{ tenths} =\blueD{2}.\greenD{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write a decimal number that is equivalent to $12$ hundredths.**

$12\text{ hundredths}=$  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Let's use a picture to help us.

Each square below is split into $100$ equal pieces, or *hundredths*. There are $\purpleC{12 \text{ hundredths}}$ shaded.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can also express $\purpleC{12 \text{ hundredths}}$ as $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}}$ and $\purpleC{2 \text{ hundredths}}$.

![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. One rectangle from the left is shaded. The remaining 9 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b62b9fbe22c88a8004682cafae3835707a703858)  ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the leftmost column, the bottom 2 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b3126ce5ed6057344dca002621d672bb9699ef78)

We can use a place value chart to write $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}}$ and $\purpleC{2 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
||$.$  | $\greenD{1}$ | $\purpleC2$
$12\text{ hundredths} = 0.\greenD{1}\purpleC2$

hints:

 ![A big square labeled 100 is divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In all 10 columns, all 10 squares are shaded..](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c35d6c4fa424592ceea435d8efa742cd3e98aeef) ![A big square labeled 100 is divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In all 10 columns, all 10 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c35d6c4fa424592ceea435d8efa742cd3e98aeef) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the leftmost column, all 10 squares are shaded. In the second column from the left, the bottom 5 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e33091afde6e550389bd4bda7ffaa8ec4c75cbec)

We can also express $\purpleC{215 \text{ hundredths}}$ as $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$, $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}}$, and $\purpleC{5 \text{ hundredths}}$.

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97111982abc11cbd025d828c5682e153d974abd4) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97111982abc11cbd025d828c5682e153d974abd4) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. One rectangle from the left is shaded. The remaining 9 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e2f9186963830e21c1e2b173bca0601dc20857e) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the leftmost column, the bottom 5 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/03a5c47e8cac2b8d3f6080637eac8e0fc6c58e3a)

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$, $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}}$, and $\purpleC{5 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD{2}$|$.$  | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleC5$
$215\text{ hundredths}  = \blueD2.\greenD1\purpleC5$

assessment_items.question_content:
**How can we write $7$ tenths and $14$ hundredths as a decimal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a picture to help us.

Each square below is split into $10$ equal pieces, or *tenths*. There are $\greenD{7 \text{ tenths}}$ shaded.

 ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Seven rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 3 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9880f7c19966ee67fa8916818794f71cee546005)

We can split the second square below into $100$ equal pieces, or *hundredths*. There are $\purpleC{14 \text{ hundredths}}$ shaded.

![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the leftmost column, all 10 squares are shaded. In the second column from the left, the bottom 4 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0bf792dfa28b622fd02b8988d4e06037b65ef860)

We can also express $\greenD{7 \text{ tenths}}$ and $\purpleC{14 \text{ hundredths}}$ as $\greenD{8 \text{ tenths}}$ and $\purpleC{4 \text{ hundredths}}$.

![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Eight rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 2 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ffd735dc774cd0eaf30e7d3fc4e52ef4923373e8) $+$ ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the leftmost column, the bottom 4 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a8c6bda1e7c4e9b656f8dfc96e84f6e0aea2259d)

We can use a place value chart to write $\greenD{8 \text{ tenths}}$ and $\purpleC{4 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | $\greenD8$ | $\purpleC4$
We can write $7\text{ tenths}$ and $14\text{ hundredths}$ as $0.\greenD8\purpleC4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How can we write $128\text{ hundredths}$ as a decimal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a picture to help us.

Each square below is split into $100$ equal pieces, or *hundredths*. There are $\purpleC{128 \text{ hundredths}}$ shaded.

 ![A big square labeled 100 is divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In all 10 columns, all 10 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e1f3a23f1f7b43b15653c15ffd668ad317cd5b9c) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the first two columns from left, all of the squares are shaded. In the third column from the left, the bottom 8 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a261a5aacbca3bacd7c6f64684bfd0747ab20b2)

We can also express $\purpleC{128 \text{ hundredths}}$ as $\blueD{1 \text{ one}}$, $\greenD{2 \text{ tenths}}$, and $\purpleC{8 \text{ hundredths}}$.

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97111982abc11cbd025d828c5682e153d974abd4)  ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Two rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 8 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fbd9f8a891847d5e141752f1d9cbfd7fe0922099) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. In the leftmost column, the bottom 8 squares are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca2c52c5eb771b390652922a294bfe9cd2613bcd)

We can use a place value chart to write $\blueD{1 \text{ one}}$, $\greenD{2 \text{ tenths}}$, and $\purpleC{8 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD{1}$|$.$  | $\greenD{2}$ | $\purpleC{8}$
We can write $128\text{ hundredths}$ as $\blueD1.\greenD2\purpleC8$.

We can also express $\purpleC{203 \text{ hundredths}}$  as $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$ and $\purpleC{3 \text{ hundredths}}$.

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97111982abc11cbd025d828c5682e153d974abd4) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97111982abc11cbd025d828c5682e153d974abd4) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 columns of 10 squares each. assessment_items.question_content:
**$2.10$ is equivalent to how many tenths?**

$2.10 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]  tenths
hints:
A place value chart shows us that $\blueD{2}.\greenD1\purpleC0$ is made up of $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$, $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}}$, and $\purpleC{0 \text{ hundredths}}$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$\blueD2$|$.$  | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleC0$

Let's use a picture to see what  $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$, $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}}$, and $\purpleC{0 \text{ hundredths}}$ looks like.

Each square below represents one whole. The last square is split into $10$ equal pieces, or *tenths*. 

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/df49c0c44f47ab8c864fd0532d30d0d1f2eeb05a) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/df49c0c44f47ab8c864fd0532d30d0d1f2eeb05a) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. One rectangle from the left is shaded. The remaining 9 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e2f9186963830e21c1e2b173bca0601dc20857e)

There are $\blueD{2 \text{ ones}}$, $\greenD{1 \text{ tenth}},$ and $\purpleC{0 \text{ hundredths}}$ shaded.

If we split each square into tenths, we see that $\greenD{21 \text{ tenths}}$ are shaded.

![A big square labeled 10 is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All 10 rectangles are shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ab06d1bc555b9d11036a936e458ba6ffcc128d02) $+$![A big square labeled 10 is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. All 10 rectangles are shaded. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ab06d1bc555b9d11036a936e458ba6ffcc128d02) $+$![A big square labeled 1 is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. One rectangle from the left is shaded. The remaining 9 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/614458ceb9bf13c504a858624aec580fcb58fc84)
$2.10 = \greenD{21 \text{ tenths}}$

 looks like.

![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/df49c0c44f47ab8c864fd0532d30d0d1f2eeb05a) ![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/df49c0c44f47ab8c864fd0532d30d0d1f2eeb05a)![A big square is completely shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/df49c0c44f47ab8c864fd0532d30d0d1f2eeb05a) ![A big square is divided into 10 equal vertical rectangles. Five rectangles from the left are shaded. The remaining 5 rectangles are not shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bd37336e21b725c3c0c71b16b6db3682b7d3c947)
$3.5 = \greenD{35 \text{ tenths}}$

|$\blueD3$|$.$  | $\greenD9$ | $\purpleC0$

$3.90 = \greenD{39 \text{ tenths}}$
